title: Inflation Hits 9.2%
briefing: Inflation hit 9.2% in June — the hottest reading since 1981 — and a $284 grocery receipt from Columbus, Ohio is going viral. You can lean on Fed Chair Diane Okafor to slam the brakes with rate hikes, or roll out relief checks that critics say will pour gas on the fire. The kitchen-table panic is real.

choices.label: Back Okafor's aggressive rate hikes
choices.detail: 75 basis points at a time, risk a slowdown.
choices.result: Prices cool over the following months, but the 30-year mortgage cracks 8% and homebuyers howl. The medicine works, slowly, and it tastes awful.

news.headline: President finally lets Okafor do her job
75-point hikes punish workers for a crisis they didn't cause
White House backs Fed tightening as inflation hits 9.2%
YOUR MORTGAGE JUST HIT 8 PERCENT 😱

social.text: finally my savings account earns more than a nickel
guess i'm renting in Tampa forever now. thanks.

choices.advisor: Okafor is right on paper, brutal politics. The pain lands before the relief shows up in polls.

choices.label: Send $1,200 relief checks
choices.result: Families cash the $1,200 checks and your approval ticks up overnight. Economists warn you've just handed inflation a second wind.

news.headline: President puts $1,200 back in working families' pockets
Helicopter money will only make prices worse
Relief checks approved as inflation debate rages
FREE MONEY DROPS — spend it FAST 💸

social.text: this check is the only reason rent got paid in Memphis this month
you cannot spend your way out of inflation. this is textbook.

choices.advisor: Instant approval bump, but you may be writing tomorrow's bad CPI print today.

title: The August Jobs Bombshell
briefing: The Labor Department just reported 18,000 new jobs — a stall — and unemployment ticked up to 5.4%. Your team wants a $220 billion public works push, but Senator Bill Hartline of West Virginia has the deficit hawks circling. You have one news cycle to shape the story.

choices.label: Launch a $220 billion works program
choices.detail: Bridges in Ohio, ports in Georgia, paychecks.
choices.result: Hard hats and ribbon-cuttings from Toledo to Savannah dominate the coverage. The deficit balloons and the 10-year Treasury yield jumps 40 basis points.

news.headline: President puts America back to work
A $220 billion jobs scheme we can't afford
White House unveils $220 billion infrastructure push
POTUS HANDS OUT HARD HATS TO AMERICA 👷

social.text: got hired on the Maumee River bridge project today. real talk this matters.
where exactly is this $220 billion coming from

choices.advisor: Photogenic and popular. Just know Hartline will hang the deficit around your neck in a few weeks.

choices.label: Trust the market to recover
choices.detail: No new spending, hold the line.
choices.result: You preach patience and fiscal discipline. Wall Street nods approvingly; the jobless in Dayton and Flint do not.

news.headline: President shows discipline, lets economy heal itself
Out-of-touch White House tells jobless to wait it out
President declines new stimulus after weak jobs report
PREZ TO UNEMPLOYED: just hang in there 🤷

social.text: 'be patient' is wild advice when rent is due friday
fiscally responsible for once. respect.

choices.advisor: The bond market loves restraint. Voters in Dayton with empty pockets won't remember your discipline fondly.

title: The Nation Grinds to a Halt
briefing: Some 340,000 rail and dock workers walked out at midnight, freezing freight from the Port of Long Beach to the Chicago yards. You can invoke emergency powers to force them back, or stand with union president Denise Calloway and let it play out. Shelves in Atlanta and Phoenix are already thinning.

choices.label: Order workers back to the job
choices.result: Freight rolls out of Long Beach within 48 hours and the shelves restock, but Calloway brands you a strikebreaker and vows revenge at the ballot box.

news.headline: President keeps $2 billion a day moving, ends chaos
Betrayal: White House crushes the rail workers' strike
Emergency order ends rail-port strike; unions outraged
PREZ DROPS THE HAMMER ON STRIKERS 🔨

social.text: we will remember this in november. count on it.
my containers move again. business survives another week.

choices.advisor: Supply chains saved, labor base scorched. Calloway will follow you for years.

choices.label: Back Calloway, broker a deal
choices.result: Calloway's members win a 24% raise over four years and hail you as a champion. The strike drags on nine more days first, and the economy eats an $18 billion hit.

news.headline: President stands with rail workers, wins them a fair deal
Caving to the unions costs the economy $18 billion
Negotiated settlement ends strike after nine costly days
WORKERS WIN BIG — bosses SEETHING 💪

social.text: FINALLY a president who has our backs
great, now every union from Detroit to Houston wants the same. lovely.

choices.advisor: You bought loyalty from labor at the cost of a bruised economy. Worth it if the recovery is quick.

title: Beijing Fires the First Shot
briefing: Beijing just slapped 25% tariffs on American soybeans, pork, and aircraft parts, and Iowa farm country is bleeding within a week. You can retaliate in kind, or take Premier Wen Zhao's quiet offer of talks in Geneva. Your factory towns want blood; your economists want peace.

choices.label: Hit back with matching tariffs
choices.result: Steel towns from Gary to Pittsburgh roar with approval as you defend them. Consumer prices climb at home and Beijing answers with a rare-earth export squeeze.

news.headline: President defends American workers from Beijing's dumping
Tariff tit-for-tat will tax every consumer
US imposes retaliatory tariffs; Beijing vows response
PREZ GOES TO WAR — over WASHING MACHINES ⚔️

social.text: about time someone stood up for our mills
everything in my store is about to cost more. cool cool cool.

choices.advisor: Flag-waving and popular, but trade wars have no winners — just slower losers. Beijing can outlast you.

choices.label: Cut a quiet deal in Geneva
choices.result: Three weeks in Geneva produce a truce: Beijing drops the tariffs, you shelve the retaliation list. Critics call it weakness; soybean futures rally 12%.

news.headline: Cool-headed Geneva deal reopens China for business
President blinks first while Iowa jobs hang in the balance
US-China trade truce reached; tariffs rolled back
POTUS SHAKES HANDS WITH BEIJING 🤝

social.text: my soybeans can finally ship to Shanghai again. huge.
weak. we should've made Beijing beg.

choices.advisor: Markets reward stability, but 'caving to Beijing' is an easy attack line. Sell it as strength.

title: The $15 Question
briefing: The Fair Wage Act — $7.25 to $15 an hour — just cleared the Senate 51-49 and landed on your desk. Workers are rallying in Lafayette Square; diner owners from Tulsa to Toledo are flooding the phones. You promised to lift wages, but the timing couldn't be tighter.

choices.label: Sign the $15 wage into law
choices.result: Twenty-eight million workers get a raise overnight and your base is ecstatic. Diners in small-town Kansas warn of cutbacks and a few storefronts go dark.

news.headline: Historic $15: President delivers a living wage
The $15 mandate will shutter the corner store
President signs $15 minimum wage into law
EVERYONE'S GETTING A RAISE 🎉 (some aren't)

social.text: my paycheck just went up 60%. i could cry.
love my staff but i literally cannot afford this. cutting hours.

choices.advisor: Legacy-defining and base-energizing. The small-town Kansas casualty stories will be the counterpunch.

choices.label: Veto and propose a slow phase-in
choices.result: You pitch $15 by 2031 to soften the blow. Businesses exhale; the crowd in Lafayette Square accuses you of betraying the moment.

news.headline: President shows restraint with phased wage plan
Half-measure betrays the workers who elected him
President vetoes wage bill, offers five-year phase-in
POTUS SAYS 'NOT SO FAST' ON RAISES ✋

social.text: phased in over five years?? i needed the raise YEARS ago
ok THIS i can plan around. thank you.

choices.advisor: The compromise protects the economy but enrages the people who wanted a win today.

title: Vantage Pacific on the Brink
briefing: Vantage Pacific Bank — the nation's third largest, with $1.9 trillion in assets — is hours from collapse, and depositors are lining up outside its San Francisco branches. A federal bailout could stop the panic from spreading, or you could let it fail and send a message about moral hazard. Tokyo markets open in six hours.

choices.label: Bail out Vantage Pacific
choices.result: Treasury guarantees every deposit and the lines outside Vantage Pacific dissolve by lunchtime. The public seethes at a $300 billion rescue for the powerful.

news.headline: Decisive rescue prevents a second 2008
Wall Street wins again while families foot the bill
Federal backstop halts run on Vantage Pacific
ANOTHER BANK BAILOUT?! who's bailing out US 🏦

social.text: my paycheck account is safe. whatever it took, thank you.
privatize the profits, socialize the losses. same as always.

choices.advisor: You stopped the bleeding but bought every 'bailout' attack ad for the next cycle.

choices.label: Let it fail
choices.result: You stand firm and the FDIC seizes Vantage Pacific by Friday night. The public cheers your spine even as regional bank stocks drop 30% and fear spreads.

news.headline: President finally ends the era of bank bailouts
Reckless inaction triggers a coast-to-coast panic
Vantage Pacific collapses as government declines rescue
BANK GOES BOOM 💥 markets in FREEFALL

social.text: let them fail like the rest of us would. good.
every regional bank is red. this is how 2008 started.

choices.advisor: Populist gold, economic gamble. If the contagion spreads past Vantage Pacific, this approval bump won't survive it.

title: Tax the Billionaires?
briefing: A 3% annual tax on fortunes over $1 billion would raise $420 billion — enough to fund your entire domestic agenda. The donor class is threatening to decamp to Monaco and your fundraisers are panicking. The public, however, is on board by 40 points.

choices.label: Impose the wealth tax
choices.result: The public roars as the first $40 billion flows to schools and clinics. Helios Motors founder Grady Voss very loudly announces he's moving to Monaco.

news.headline: At last, the ultra-rich pay their fair share
The $420 billion tax will drive capital to Zurich
Wealth tax enacted; capital-flight concerns raised
BILLIONAIRES RAGE-QUIT THE COUNTRY 🛫

social.text: you mean they weren't already paying this?? sign me up
great, my accountant already booked the flight to monaco

choices.advisor: Wildly popular and legacy-rich. Expect a wave of 'Voss is fleeing' headlines — get ahead of it.

choices.label: Drop the tax, court investment
choices.result: You reassure the markets and Voss's crowd breathes easy. Your base accuses you of folding to the billionaire class.

news.headline: President protects investment and keeps capital home
Caving to billionaires over working families
Wealth tax proposal shelved amid investment concerns
PREZ LETS BILLIONAIRES OFF THE HOOK 💰

social.text: knew it. always us, never them.
smart. markets reward stability and you just delivered.

choices.advisor: Markets calm, base furious. You traded a popular fight for economic steadiness.

title: The Housing Crisis Boils Over
briefing: Rents in Austin, Phoenix, and Nashville have doubled in five years, and a tent encampment just sprang up on the National Mall across from the Capitol. You can push sweeping rent control with a building fund, or unleash developers by gutting zoning rules. Either way, someone's getting priced out of the conversation.

choices.label: Cap rents and fund building
choices.result: Renters celebrate immediate relief, but landlords in Phoenix and Miami pull units off the market and new construction stalls in a dozen metros.

news.headline: President takes on greedy landlords for renters
Rent caps will dry up housing supply for good
Rent-control package signed amid supply warnings
RENT FROZEN — tenants DANCING in the streets 🏠

social.text: my landlord can't jack the rent again. i can finally breathe.
i'm selling and converting. enjoy the shortage.

choices.advisor: Tenants are a huge bloc and they'll love this. Watch for the 'Phoenix supply collapse' story in six months.

choices.label: Slash rules, let developers build
choices.result: Cranes go up from Raleigh to Boise and supply expands over time. Neighborhood groups protest the loss of zoning protections and green space.

news.headline: President unleashes a building boom
Developer giveaway bulldozes neighborhoods
Zoning rollback aims to boost housing supply
BULLDOZERS INCOMING — say bye to your park 🏗️

social.text: we can actually break ground now. game changer.
they're putting a 20-story tower next to my house. cool.

choices.advisor: Supply is the long-term fix, but the short-term anger is loud and local. The relief is slow.

title: AtlasCoin Goes to Zero
briefing: AtlasCoin, the $580 billion token anchoring half the crypto market, went to zero overnight and dragged the broader market down 4%. Millions of small investors are wiped out and demanding action. You can launch a sweeping crackdown, or backstop the wreckage to calm the markets.

choices.label: Launch a sweeping crackdown
choices.result: You bring the hammer down: exchanges must register, tokens need audited reserves, and the worst actors flee. Founders warn the whole industry will decamp to Dubai and Singapore.

news.headline: President protects everyday investors from crypto cons
Heavy-handed rules will chase innovation to Singapore
Sweeping crypto rules announced after AtlasCoin collapse
CRYPTO BROS IN SHAMBLES 📉 regulators POUNCE

social.text: lost my savings to AtlasCoin. glad someone's finally acting.
regulating us to death. we'll just build in Dubai.

choices.advisor: Protective and popular with the burned. The 'killing innovation' line is the predictable counter.

choices.label: Backstop the market, stay hands-off
choices.detail: Calm the panic, keep it free.
choices.result: You inject confidence and the broader markets claw back the 4% slide. Critics blast you for rescuing a casino and changing nothing about it.

news.headline: President steadies markets without strangling innovation
Another rescue for speculators, none for their victims
Federal move stabilizes markets after AtlasCoin collapse
POTUS SAVES THE CRYPTO CASINO 🎰

social.text: markets stopped bleeding. that's all i needed.
you bailed out the gamblers but not the people they ruined?

choices.advisor: Markets steadied, public unconvinced. 'Bailing out the casino' writes itself — have a rebuttal ready.
